summary: BUILD-VS-REUSE VERDICT: reuse TalentOS as the ranking service, do NOT rebuild (rebuilding is ng4 and blows c2). Couple LOOSELY via its REST API behind a thin adapter: an ATS 'job' maps 1:1 to a TalentOS 'project'; on resume dump the ATS calls create-project -> PUT job -> POST resumes -> POST /process -> POST /rank, then caches the returned RankingResult in the ATS DB. Stack: single web service + Postgres (pipeline/stages/notes/users own the source of truth; TalentOS owns ranking only). TalentOS runs as a local sidecar (c5). The integration + client view (f7) will NOT both fit c2 — agree with PM's timeline escalation.

architecture.shape: Single ATS web service (FastAPI) + Postgres + React frontend (tm1 skills). TalentOS as a separate local service reached over HTTP through one adapter module.
architecture.integration_contract: ats_job.talentos_project_id links the two. Dump flow is async: enqueue-free, a single background task per dump calls /process then /rank (cron/inline, not a queue — volume doesn't justify one). RankingResult (score, evidence, strengths/gaps, explanation) is cached on the ATS candidate row so the board renders without re-hitting TalentOS.
architecture.data_ownership: ATS DB = jobs, candidates, stages, notes, users, contact-locks (f6). TalentOS = embeddings, ranking, evidence. Resume files stored once; pushed to TalentOS, referenced from ATS.
architecture.adapter_rationale: The thin adapter isolates r1 (a1 is unversioned): if TalentOS's API shifts, one module changes. A contract test pins the response shape.

gaps_to_clarify.text: Client auth model (gap1) determines whether f7 is a shared service with a second authz realm or a separate read-only app — affects the whole f7 estimate.

challenges_to_brief.concern: TalentOS integration (adapter + dump orchestration + result caching) is itself ~1 week; a shared board + double-contact guard is another; f7 with its own auth surface is a third. 'A few weeks' fits ~two of those three.
challenges_to_brief.recommendation: Same escalation as PM: recruiter-only MVP first, or extend timeline. I can hit c2 for f1-f6; not f1-f7.

challenges_to_brief.concern: r1 is real but mitigable and shouldn't block: the adapter + a contract test contains it. Flagging so it isn't treated as a reason to fork/rebuild TalentOS (which would be worse).
challenges_to_brief.recommendation: Accept r1 with the adapter mitigation; keep reuse.

complexity_justification.recommendation: Run TalentOS as a separate service the ATS calls (a two-service topology).
complexity_justification.why_needed: cites brief.constraints.c4 — TalentOS already exists and provides g2 end-to-end; and brief.non_goals.ng4 — building ranking is explicitly out of scope.
complexity_justification.simpler_alternative_considered: Embed ranking logic directly inside the ATS as one process.
complexity_justification.why_rejected: Embedding would duplicate a working system (violates ng4), require porting TalentOS's FAISS/LLM pipeline, and break c2. Reusing the existing service IS the simpler path to satisfy the Brief, despite being two processes.

complexity_justification.why_needed: cites brief.goals.g1 (multiple recruiters on one shared board = concurrent writes) and brief.goals.g4 (external client reads).
complexity_justification.why_rejected: Concurrent multi-recruiter writes on a shared pipeline + an external reader make SQLite's single-writer model a real limitation, not a hypothetical one. Within the internal-tool ceiling, which prefers Postgres.
